zh.lifeSummary: Seigneur De Barbençon Jean 1er De Barbançon是Nicolas II De Barbençon和Isabeau De Soissons-Nesie的兒子，於1312年出生。他和Marie d'Antoing至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Seigneur De Barbençon Jean 1er De Barbançon是Nicolas II De Barbençon和Isabeau De Soissons-Nesie的儿子，于1312年出生。他和Marie d'Antoing至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: 1er
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: Seigneur De Barbençon Jean 1er De Barbançon was born in 1312 as the son of Nicolas II De Barbençon and Isabeau De Soissons-Nesie. He had at least 1 son with Marie d'Antoing.

fr.lifeSummary: Seigneur De Barbençon Jean 1er De Barbançon est né en 1312, fils de Nicolas II De Barbençon et Isabeau De Soissons-Nesie. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Marie d'Antoing.

de.lifeSummary: Seigneur De Barbençon Jean 1er De Barbançon wurde 1312, als Sohn von Nicolas II De Barbençon und Isabeau De Soissons-Nesie geboren. Er hatte mit Marie d'Antoing mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Seigneur De Barbençon Jean 1er De Barbançon è nato il nel 1312 come figlio di Nicolas II De Barbençon e Isabeau De Soissons-Nesie. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Marie d'Antoing.

ja.lifeSummary: Seigneur De Barbençon Jean 1er De Barbançonは1312年にNicolas II De BarbençonとIsabeau De Soissons-Nesieの息子として生まれた。彼はMarie d'Antoingとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Seigneur De Barbençon Jean 1er De Barbançon(은)는 1312년 Nicolas II De Barbençon(와)과 Isabeau De Soissons-Nesie의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Marie d'Antoing(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Seigneur De Barbençon Jean 1er De Barbançon nasceu em 1312 filho de Nicolas II De Barbençon e Isabeau De Soissons-Nesie. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Marie d'Antoing.

ru.lifeSummary: Seigneur De Barbençon Jean 1er De Barbançon родился в 1312 г. Его родителей звали Nicolas II De Barbençon и Isabeau De Soissons-Nesie. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Marie d'Antoing.

es.lifeSummary: Seigneur De Barbençon Jean 1er De Barbançon nació en 1312 como hijo de Nicolas II De Barbençon y Isabeau De Soissons-Nesie. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Marie d'Antoing.
